_description: Rules applied per question for Australia vs Egypt 2026-07-03

match_context.rule8_note: Draw 33.78% >= 25%. Most relevant for Q13 (offsides, thin-sample TEAM_MODEL) and Q9 (Egypt SOT, thin-sample TEAM_MODEL).

per_question.note: Trezeguet (Smarkets contract 'Mahmoud Hassan') anytime-scorer offer=0.2222 x0.945=0.210. Did not start any group game (single 76'-sub appearance vs NZL); question implies he's a live R32 selection. Trust market's illiquid-adjusted price over trying to build a from-scratch role-based prior.
FTR Egypt mid=0.4048. Cross-validated against independent DraftKings pickcenter (vig-normalized EGY=40.2%) -- near-exact agreement, high confidence.
HT result Draw mid=0.5063. High HT-draw price is consistent with RULE8's elevated match-level draw signal (33.78%).
OU2.5 Under mid=0.6714 = P(0,1, or 2 goals). Combined lambda ~2.05-2.12 supports a moderate-low-scoring lean.

Irankunda SOT OU1.5 Over: bid=0.069, offer=0.1562, mid=0.1126. Spread (8.7pp) is unusually wide for a 'liquid' market -- treated with caution, shaded slightly up toward the offer-implied illiquid convention (offer x0.945=0.1476). Blend of mid and shaded-offer -> 0.13. Consistent with his rotational/impact-sub role in group stage (limited sustained shot volume).

P(AUS>=1 card)=1-e^-1.667=0.811 x P(EGY>=1 card)=1-e^-2.0=0.865 = 0.701. Both teams averaged high card counts in group stage (AUS 1.67/game incl. a 4-YC game vs USA; EGY 2.0/game). Same methodology as POR-CRO Q7 (0.55) -- higher here because both teams' own card rates are individually higher.
Cards OU3.5 Over mid=0.3770, tight spread (0.357-0.397). Consistent with Poisson(lambda=3.05) fit -> P(>=4)=0.364. Use market directly.
P(pen OR red) = P(pen=0.2487) + P(red=0.1409) - P(both)=0.2487x0.1409 = 0.3546. Penalty market illiquid (offer-only), red-card market liquid and tight.

Egypt SOT market (OU3.5, thin/wide spread) fits lambda=3.598 -> P(>=5)=0.293. Egypt's own group-stage SOT average (3,7,3=4.33) as lambda -> P(>=5)=0.436. Market likely underweights Egypt's dominant-possession current form; ESPN empirical likely overweights the NZL blowout outlier. Blended lambda=3.966 -> P(>=5)=0.364.
Corners OU8.5 Over side has no offer quote; Under side is fully quoted (mid=0.5079) -> implied Over=0.4921. Cross-check via Poisson(lambda=8.71 fit from OU7.5/8.5/9.5)=0.505. Blended ~0.49.
Team-to-score-first Egypt mid=0.4654. Consistent with Egypt's higher win probability (40.5%) and higher-shots-volume group-stage profile.
Highest-scoring-half Second Half contract mid=0.3989. Same DIRECT methodology used successfully in MEX-ECU Q2 (+beat crowd).

No market. Own-team-only combined offside lambda=0.667 (AUS 0.33 + EGY 0.33 avg) gives raw P(>=3)=0.030 -- too extreme given n=3 per team. Match-environment lambda (avg of AUS-games-combined 1.67 and EGY-games-combined 2.33 = 2.0) gives P(>=3)=0.323, matching MEX-ECU's successfully-used methodology (that match also fitted combined_lambda=2.0). Historical base rate for this exact combined-total-offsides question type across settled matches (MEX-ECU=NO, GER-PAR=YES, ENG-CDR=YES) is 2/3 YES, arguing against pricing too low. Final estimate anchored to the match-environment lambda reading.

No market; new question type with no prior precedent file. ESPN keyEvents across 6 group games: only 2/6 had a genuine substitution before the 45' mark (Egypt vs NZL 41', Egypt vs Iran 14'), and BOTH were injury-forced, not tactical. Neither team shows a pattern of proactive early tactical subs. Priced modestly below the raw 33% sample rate since the 2 positive instances were exogenous (injury) events, not a repeatable tendency.

Total shots lambda=23.06 (fit from OU23.5 Over=0.4502) -> P(>=20)=0.766. Cross-checked against ESPN combined average (AUS 8.67 + EGY 16.0 = 24.67 shots/game), consistent direction. High confidence -- Egypt alone averages 16 shots/game.
